keyEvents.impact: National humiliation; enduring determination never to depend on others for survival
De Gaulle's strong presidency; executive dominance in foreign and defense policy

keyEvents.event: First nuclear test in the Sahara
keyEvents.impact: Independent force de frappe; deterrence answering to Paris alone

keyEvents.event: Algerian independence after eight years of war
keyEvents.impact: End of empire; pivot to influence networks in Africa rather than direct rule

keyEvents.event: Withdrawal from NATO integrated military command
keyEvents.impact: Gaullist strategic autonomy; ally of the West but never a subordinate
Bet on European integration as a multiplier of French power
Intensified counterterrorism interventions in the Sahel and Middle East

history.narrative: A former great power that compensates for relative decline with nuclear independence, a permanent Security Council seat, and an activist military posture from the Sahel to the Mediterranean. Pursues 'strategic autonomy' for itself and Europe: allied with Washington but allergic to subordination, and convinced that France must speak with its own voice in every crisis.

redLines.description: Armed attack on an EU or NATO member state
Threat to French overseas territories or citizens abroad
Any attempt to constrain or neutralize the independent French deterrent
Hostile power dominating the Mediterranean or French interests in Africa

internalDivisions.name: Laicite and integration tensions in the banlieues

goals.description: Maintain independent foreign policy and a credible sovereign nuclear deterrent
Lead European strategic autonomy and a stronger EU defense pillar
Preserve influence and counterterrorism reach in Africa and the Mediterranean
Expand defense and aerospace exports to sustain the industrial base
